title: Atlas Outpost Graybox Navigation
summary: Finish a Windows GPU Blender/Godot graybox level blockout so the exported Atlas Outpost layout supports the required visible navigation routes and review constraints.
taskPrompt:
You are working on a Windows GPU VM.

## Variant
`base`: Atlas Outpost graybox navigation blockout

## Your Task
Finish the Atlas Outpost graybox level-design starter project. This is a level-blockout workflow: author route geometry, re-export the glTF, wire the Godot validation scene, and write the visible handoff metrics.

## Input Files
- Task prompt: `base\input\TASK_PROMPT.md`
- Starter project root: `base\input`
- Blender blockout: `base\input\blender\atlas_outpost_blockout.blend`
- Exported glTF starter: `base\input\exports\atlas_outpost_blockout.gltf`
- Godot validation scene: `base\input\godot\scenes\atlas_outpost_validation.tscn`
- Optional Blender launcher: `base\software\open_blender.bat`
- Optional Godot launcher: `base\software\open_godot.bat`

## What You Must Do
1. Read `base\input\TASK_PROMPT.md` and the visible specs under `base\input`.
2. Complete the graybox route layout in the project files.
3. Re-export `exports/atlas_outpost_blockout.gltf` and any referenced sidecar files.
4. Update `godot/scenes/atlas_outpost_validation.tscn` with the required review markers.
5. Fill `docs/layout_handoff.md` with the visible route metrics.
6. Save the completed project under `base\output` using the same relative paths as the starter.

## Required Output Paths
- `base\output\blender\atlas_outpost_blockout.blend`
- `base\output\exports\atlas_outpost_blockout.gltf`
- `base\output\exports\atlas_outpost_blockout.bin` if referenced by the exported glTF
- `base\output\godot\scenes\atlas_outpost_validation.tscn`
- `base\output\docs\layout_handoff.md`

## Important
- Keep your final deliverables under `base\output`.
- Do not modify `input\` or any non-output data.
- Do not rely on internet access.
agentMustDo: Read the visible task prompt and starter project under `input/`.
Complete the Blender/Godot graybox project and exported glTF route geometry.
Update the Godot validation scene and visible handoff notes.
Save the completed project with the same relative paths under `output/`.

inputFiles.name: TASK_PROMPT.md
inputFiles.description: Agent-visible task brief with visible route, zone, cover, and LOS requirements.

referenceFiles.name: Completed Atlas Outpost project
referenceFiles.description: Required completed project written by the benchmarked agent.

evaluation: The evaluator checks required output file existence, referenced glTF sidecars, route lengths and zone budgets from named glTF nodes, cover and line-of-sight facts, Godot scene markers, and visible route metrics in `docs/layout_handoff.md`. The returned benchmark score is binary: `1.0` only if the submitted project passes the hard gates and threshold, otherwise `0.0`.

classifier.rationale: The task involves level design and navigation in a game environment, which fits within 3D animation and interactive media.
